hypothesis: If metal lattices display strong electron localization at interstitial quasi-atoms, three-dimensional electronic connectivity, high hydrogen content, and significant hydrogen-derived density of states at the Fermi level, then they will form thermodynamically stable superhydrides with strong electron-phonon coupling and high superconducting transition temperatures, and a stacked AutoGluon machine-learning model trained on lattice-derived features can accurately identify these candidates prior to DFT validation.
